name: whisper.cpp
description: C/C++ local Whisper runtime for offline, on-device transcription

developer.comment: Community-driven open-source project under the ggml-org organization, originally authored by Georgi Gerganov.

first-release.comment: Repository created 2022-09-25 (project open-sourced that month). Earliest tagged release available via the GitHub API is v1.0.4 on 2022-12-17; the project itself dates to September 2022.

status.comment: Actively maintained; recent v1.8.x releases through 2024.

primary-type.comment: A runnable C/C++ inference runtime (CLI + library) for OpenAI's Whisper model that a technical user drives themselves; its core purpose is transcription via that engine.

real-time-dictation.comment: Not a speak-to-type productivity tool. It can produce a near-real-time microphone transcript via the bundled stream example, but does not inject text into other apps.

file-transcription.comment: Primary use: transcribe audio files (WAV; other formats via ffmpeg).

live-meeting-capture.comment: No meeting bot or built-in conference/system-audio capture; only direct microphone or files.

streaming-low-latency.comment: The bundled 'stream' example produces a running transcript of live microphone audio in near-real-time (experimental, sliding-window).

audio-sources.comment: Microphone via the stream example; files via the main CLI. No native meeting-bot or system-audio capture.

underlying-engine.comment: It is a C/C++ port of OpenAI's Whisper ASR model running on the ggml tensor library; uses the official Whisper model weights (tiny through large).

language-count.comment: Approx; inherits Whisper's ~99 supported languages. Effective coverage depends on the model size loaded.

english-accuracy.comment: Runs the same Whisper weights as the reference model; large-v3 is near-human on clean English (independent WER typically ~2-4%). Rated 4 rather than 5 because accuracy depends heavily on which model size the user loads (tiny/base are notably weaker).

multilingual-auto-detect.comment: Automatic language detection (Whisper feature); also supports forcing a language.

custom-vocabulary.comment: Supports an initial/context prompt (--prompt) that can be used to bias recognition toward specific terms and spellings; not a persistent dictionary UI but a real user-facing mechanism.

speaker-diarization.comment: Experimental speaker segmentation via tinydiarize (-tdrz flag), which marks speaker turns; limited compared to named multi-speaker diarization.

timestamps.comment: Segment-level timestamps by default; experimental word-level timestamps via -ml 1.

ai-summaries-notes.comment: Produces transcripts only; no built-in summarization or notes.

built-in-editor.comment: Command-line/library tool with no transcript-editing UI.

translation.comment: Whisper's translate mode can output English from non-English audio (translation to English only, not arbitrary target languages).

export-formats.comment: CLI flags -otxt, -osrt, -ovtt, -oj. Also supports CSV (-ocsv) and LRC outputs, which have no tag in the defined set.

processing-location.comment: Fully local inference; no cloud component.

offline-capable.comment: Runs entirely offline once the model weights are downloaded.

data-used-for-training.comment: Audio never leaves the device; there is no vendor service to collect or train on user data.

data-deletion-control.comment: All inputs and outputs are local files fully under the user's control; no server-side retention.

platforms.comment: Runs on macOS (Intel/ARM), Windows (MSVC/MinGW), Linux, iOS, Android, and in the browser via WebAssembly; also FreeBSD/Raspberry Pi.

system-wide-dictation.comment: Does not inject text into other apps OS-wide; it is an engine/CLI, not a dictation front-end.

integrations.comment: No meeting/app integrations; intended to be embedded or scripted by developers.

api-automation.comment: C-style API plus command-line tools and bindings for Rust, Go, Java, Ruby, Python, JavaScript, .NET and more.

pricing-model.comment: MIT open-source; free to use. Only practical cost is local compute and setup effort.

free-allowance.comment: No usage limits; runs on the user's own hardware.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Open-source with no subscription; no monthly price applies.
